meaning: n.皈依者vt.使转变,转换...,使...改变信仰
examples: This requires open investments to help doctors convert.
She, too, was a convert to Roman Catholicism.
The indies convert their digital movies to film.
To convert, you declare the entire balance in the IRA as taxable income and pay tax on it.
So far, at least, Packard has been able to convert them to his gospel of self-reliance and penny-pinching.
